title: AgileMarkdown Item
description: YAML frontmatter for a backlog item (.md file). The frontmatter block sits at the top of the file between two `---` lines. The markdown body follows.

type.description: Story type. Only `feature` (and optionally `bug`/`chore` per project config) feeds velocity. `release` is a date marker.

status.description: Workflow state. unstarted -> started -> finished -> delivered -> accepted (or -> rejected -> started).

assigned.description: One or more assignees. A scalar string is the single-owner form (back-compat with v4.2 and earlier); a YAML sequence carries up to three names for shared work.

blocked.description: True when the story is blocked. Renders a blocked badge in the VS Code extension and in any MCP client.

blocked_reason.description: Optional reason text paired with `blocked: true`.

hypothesis.description: Legacy. Acceptance criteria live in the story body under `## Acceptance` (Pivotal way). This field is kept for back-compat and is no longer used by the coach. See `am explain acceptance-criteria`.

epic.description: Slug of the epic this story belongs to. Used by `am show epic <slug>` to roll up burnup progress across all stories sharing the slug.

estimate.description: Story-point estimate. Must match the project's estimation scale (linear / fibonacci / powers / custom).

started.description: Set automatically when status first transitions to `started`. Used to compute cycle time (started → accepted) and the in-flight band of the cumulative-flow chart.

accepted.description: Set automatically when status transitions to `accepted`. Only this timestamp drives velocity bucketing.

release_date.description: Required for `type: release`. Target ship date used to project go/no-go from velocity.
